params.name: Death of the Virgin
params.description:
On Feb 10, 1606 AD, Caravaggio handed over The Death of the Virgin to the commissioned priests.
Death of the Virgin is an altarpiece created by Caravaggio for the church of Santa Maria della Scala. In this painting, he deviated from the traditional depiction of the Virgin, replacing the usual sacred scene of the Virgin's Ascending with the sad scene of parting and death. The priests who commissioned the painting refused it, claiming that the Virgin was depicted unattractively. However, what t …
